figures.caption: Table 12: Sampling parameters for different models
Table 7: Distribution of error categories among failed test cases on CODESPECBENCH-Func.
Figure 5: Distribution of prompt token lengths for CODESPECBENCH-Repo, computed with the Qwen3 tokenizer. Each bar shows the proportion of inputs falling within a specific token length range.
Table 11: Postcondition pass rates (%) for 61 algorithmic domains under GPT-5-mini.

Table 1: Comparison of CODESPECBENCH with representative specification-generation benchmarks along key dimensions, including granularity (function vs. repository), specification coverage (preconditions and postconditions), verification paradigm (static vs. execution-based), specification language and representation.
Figure 4: Example of a generated executable behavioral specification from natural language issue description and relevant repository code, sourced from CODESPECBENCH-Repo.

Table 4: Results on the CODESPECBENCH-Func and CODESPECBENCH-Repo benchmarks (percentages). Correct, Complete, and Pass report correctness, completeness, and pass rate under the benchmark’s execution-based evaluation protocol, respectively. Reasoning indicates whether the thinking mode is enabled.

Figure 2: Overview of generation and evaluation in CODESPECBENCH. In CODESPECBENCH-Func (top), the specification is generated from a natural language problem description (top left) and executed on correct and incorrect test cases to evaluate correctness and completeness (top right). In CODESPECBENCH-Repo (bottom), the specification is generated from a natural language issue description and relevant repository code (bottom left). Correctness is evaluated using trigger and regression tests on the fixed repository, while completeness is evaluated using trigger tests on the buggy repository (bottom right).
Table 9: Fraction-based evaluation results on the CODESPECBENCH-Func and CODESPECBENCH-Repo benchmarks (percentages).
Table 10: Full experimental results showing relationship between executable specification rate (Executable) and pass rate (Pass) on CODESPECBENCH-Repo (values in %).
Table 8: Distribution of failure types on CODESPECBENCH-Repo. Claude refers to Claude4.5-Sonnet and DeepSeek refers to DeepSeek-V3.2.
